mnemonics: TRUST: Traffic, Reputation, Unlocks, Security, Trust. This sequence describes the core principle: analyzing Traffic to establish a Reputation score, which Unlocks adaptive Security policies, building a trusted environment.
keyPoints: Moves beyond simple allow/deny rules to a dynamic, adaptive security model.
Uses continuous monitoring of behavior and traffic patterns to assign reputation scores.
Enables automated mitigation, like throttling or isolating low-reputation entities before an attack occurs.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with traditional blacklists/whitelists (it's dynamic, not static).
Assuming it only applies to external IPs (it can also monitor internal servers/VMs for lateral movement).
